steps.title: Know the condition
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has no solution when the equations represent parallel lines: the variable coefficients are proportional, but the constants are not.

steps.title: Test Choice D — $-2x+3y=9$ $4x-6y=18$
steps.content: Multiply the first equation in Choice D — $-2x+3y=9$ $4x-6y=18$ by $-2$: $$-2(-2x+3y)=-2(9)$$ $$4x-6y=-18$$ The second equation in Choice D — $-2x+3y=9$ $4x-6y=18$ is $4x-6y=18$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice D — $-2x+3y=9$ $4x-6y=18$ gives the same left side equal to two different constants, so the lines are parallel and never intersect. D.
